get.description: Returns 200 with `status: "ok"` when DB and Redis are both reachable. Returns 503 with `status: "degraded"` if either dependency fails. Response is bare JSON — bypasses the standard envelope so monitoring tools (Railway healthcheck, UptimeRobot) see a simple shape.

post.description: Backend does NOT send the SMS — Firebase phone auth is initiated by the Flutter client via the firebase_auth SDK. This endpoint exists so the backend can rate-limit per phone (3 requests / 10 minutes) and keep an audit trail.
Client passes the Firebase ID token obtained after the user enters the OTP. Backend verifies the token via Firebase Admin SDK, creates a `users` row if new, and returns an access + refresh JWT pair.
post.summary: Verify Firebase ID token, return JWT pair

post.description: The client then triggers the Firebase phone-OTP SMS itself (firebase_auth SDK). Errors: PHONE_ALREADY_REGISTERED (409).
post.summary: Begin signup — reject taken phones, return a signup ticket

post.description: Pass the ticket from /signup/start plus the Firebase ID token. Errors: SIGNUP_TICKET_INVALID (400), OTP_INVALID (400).
post.summary: Verify the Firebase OTP token, return a single-use signup token

post.description: Errors: SIGNUP_TOKEN_EXPIRED/INVALID (400), VALIDATION_ERROR (400), EMAIL_ALREADY_USED (409), PHONE_ALREADY_REGISTERED (409).
post.summary: Finish signup — create the user, return a JWT pair

post.description: Errors: INVALID_CREDENTIALS (401), PASSWORD_NOT_SET (400), ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED/BANNED (403), RATE_LIMITED (429 after 5 failures / 5 min).
Returns a reset ticket. The client then triggers the Firebase OTP SMS.
post.summary: Request a password reset — always 200 (no registration leak)

post.description: Errors: RESET_TICKET_INVALID (400), OTP_INVALID (400), VALIDATION_ERROR (400).
post.summary: Reset password with OTP, revoke all sessions, return a fresh JWT pair

post.description: Errors: INVALID_CREDENTIALS (401), PASSWORD_NOT_SET (400), VALIDATION_ERROR (400).
post.summary: Change password — revoke all sessions, return a fresh JWT pair

post.description: Errors: PASSWORD_ALREADY_SET (400), VALIDATION_ERROR (400).

post.summary: Rotate refresh token, return new JWT pair
Revoke every session except the current one

get.description: Vehicle types, support contacts, legal URLs, supported languages, Razorpay key id, and version/force-update info. The app pulls this on launch and after every login, and caches it for 6h.

post.description: For ADMIN / SUPPORT users only. Verifies argon2id password and returns the same JWT-pair shape as the rider OTP flow. Riders / drivers do NOT use this endpoint — they authenticate via /auth/otp/verify.

post.description: Idempotent — returns the existing profile if one is already set up. Also adds the DRIVER role to the user so subsequent driver endpoints succeed.

Field name: `file`. Pass `docType` (and optional `docNumber`, `expiresAt`) as query params. Accepted MIME types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp, application/pdf. Max 5MB. Re-uploading the same docType replaces the previous file and resets verification.

parameters.name: expiresAt
parameters.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD). Used for DL / insurance / permit expiry.

get.summary: List the current driver’s KYC documents
Return overall KYC status + missing required docs

post.description: Sets status=ONLINE and registers the driver in the live Redis geo pool (once a location is known). The vehicle must be owned by the driver and ACTIVE.
Removes the driver from the live Redis pool and sets status=OFFLINE.
Refreshes the Redis geo entry on every call; the durable Postgres write is debounced to once every 5s. Requires the driver to be ONLINE or ON_TRIP.
Call after sign-in and whenever firebase_messaging issues a new token. Idempotent — a re-register updates `lastSeenAt` rather than inserting a duplicate.
post.summary: Register / refresh the current device’s FCM token
Unregister the given FCM token (typically on sign-out)

post.description: Driver profile must already exist (POST /drivers/me/profile). Registration number is unique across the platform and locked once set.

post.description: Field name: `photo`. Accepted types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/webp. Max 5MB.

get.description: Results cached in Redis for 24h. Biased to India.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text place query to resolve into coordinates.

get.summary: Forward geocode — resolve a free-text query to candidate places

get.description: Results cached in Redis for 24h. Returns 404 if no match.

parameters.name: lat
parameters.description: Latitude, -90..90 (WGS84).

parameters.name: lng
parameters.description: Longitude, -180..180 (WGS84).

get.summary: Reverse geocode — resolve a coordinate to a single place

post.description: Returns distance, duration, an encoded polyline, and the snapped waypoints. Results cached in Redis for 1h. 404 with NO_ROUTE when the provider finds no path.

Routes the trip via the configured routing provider, applies the active pricing rule for (vehicleType, KOLKATA), and returns the breakdown + total in integer paise. 404 NO_ROUTE if no route exists; 404 PRICING_NOT_FOUND if no rule is active.
post.summary: Upfront fare estimate for a trip

post.description: Routes the trip once and returns a fare per priced vehicle type (BIKE/AUTO/CNG/CAR) for the fare quote sheet. 404 NO_ROUTE if no route exists; 404 PRICING_NOT_FOUND if no vehicle type has an active pricing rule.
post.summary: Upfront fare estimate for all vehicle types at once

post.description: Computes a fare snapshot, creates a PENDING ride request, and enqueues the async driver-matching job. Returns 404 if no route or pricing rule applies.

get.summary: Get a ride request by public id (must be the owner)

post.description: Allowed only while the request is PENDING or MATCHED.
Atomically claims the offer and creates the trip. First accepter wins — a losing race or a stale/expired offer returns 409.
Marks the offer DECLINED so matching advances to the next driver immediately.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by trip status

get.description: Lets the rider app resume the trip screen on relaunch. 404 NO_ACTIVE_TRIP if none.
Full trip detail. Accessible to the rider, the driver, or admin/support.

post.description: ACCEPTED → ARRIVED. Driver-only. 409 if the trip is not ACCEPTED.
ARRIVED → STARTED. Driver-only. The driver must enter the 4-digit `otp` the rider shows them: 400 OTP_REQUIRED if missing, 409 OTP_INVALID if wrong. 409 if the trip is not ARRIVED.
STARTED → ENDED. Driver-only. Finalizes total_fare + fare_breakdown and releases the driver back online. 409 if not STARTED.
Rider or driver, only before the trip STARTS (ACCEPTED or ARRIVED). Records who cancelled + the reason. 409 once STARTED.
Rider-only, after the trip has ENDED. Records the rating + comment and updates the driver rating average. 409 if not ended or already rated.
Driver-only, after the trip has ENDED. 409 if not ended or already rated.
Either party (rider or driver) can file a report against a trip they were on.
post.summary: Report a problem with a trip

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by trip status

get.description: Lets the driver app resume on relaunch. 404 NO_ACTIVE_TRIP if none.

post.description: Rider-only. Returns the gateway order id the Razorpay SDK opens the sheet with.

post.description: Rider-only. Verifies the client-returned signature; on success marks the payment PAID and credits the driver wallet. 422 PAYMENT_FAILED if invalid.

post.description: Driver-only, CASH trips. Closes the payment and debits the platform commission + GST from the driver wallet.

info.title: Ride-Sharing API
info.description: Kolkata ride-sharing backend — on-demand (Uber-style) + scheduled carpool (BlaBlaCar-style). All responses follow the envelope in docs/API_CONVENTIONS.md.

phone.description: Phone number in E.164 format (with country code).

cooldownSeconds.description: Cooldown before another /otp/send call is permitted for this phone.

note.description: Note for Flutter devs: backend does not send the SMS itself. The Flutter client should call Firebase phone auth via firebase_auth SDK to receive the OTP. This endpoint exists for backend-side rate limiting and audit.

idToken.description: Firebase ID token returned by the client SDK after the user enters the OTP. Backend verifies this via Firebase Admin SDK.

phone.description: Phone number in E.164 format (with country code).

firebaseIdToken.description: Firebase ID token the Flutter client obtains after the user enters the OTP (firebase_auth SDK). Backend verifies it and confirms the phone matches the ticket. Backend does NOT receive the raw OTP — Firebase phone auth is client-side.

expiresInSec.description: Seconds until the signup token expires.

signupToken.description: Single-use signup token from /auth/signup/verify-otp (10 min TTL).

password.description: Exactly 6 digits (numeric). Keypad-friendly for daily login.

resetTicket.description: Always returned (even for unknown phones) so the API never leaks who is registered.

firebaseIdToken.description: Firebase ID token obtained after the user enters the OTP (firebase_auth SDK). Backend verifies it and confirms the phone matches the reset ticket.

refreshToken.description: Refresh token issued by /auth/otp/verify or a prior /auth/refresh.
Refresh token to revoke. Access token is revoked when it expires.
The caller's current refresh token — the one session to keep.

current.description: True for the caller's own session.

iconUrl.description: Optional icon URL. Flutter may ship its own assets and ignore this.

razorpayKeyId.description: Razorpay publishable key id (may be empty).

forceUpdate.description: When true, the client must show a blocking update screen regardless of the installed version. Also flips on automatically (client-side) when the installed version is below minSupportedVersion.

docNumber.description: Decrypted for admin display. May be null if not provided on upload.

fileUrl.description: Signed URL valid for 1 hour. Re-fetch the driver detail to get a fresh URL.

reason.example: Aadhaar photo blurry — please re-upload a clearer copy.

driverUserId.description: Numeric driver user id (matches the WS event key).

locationUpdatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of the last position write.

platformFeePct.description: Platform fee percent (0–100). Defaults to 10 if omitted.

gstPct.description: GST percent (0–100). Defaults to 5 if omitted.

vehicleId.description: Public ID of the vehicle the driver is going online with. Must be ACTIVE and owned by the driver.
Public ID of the vehicle the driver is currently online with, or null.

location.description: Last reported position, or null if the driver has never pinged a location.

locationUpdatedAt.description: ISO timestamp of the last location ping, or null.

wentOnlineAt.description: ISO timestamp the driver last went online, or null.

bearing.description: Heading in degrees, 0–360 clockwise from north.

fcmToken.description: Raw FCM registration token from firebase_messaging on the device.

deviceInfo.description: Free-form metadata (model, OS version, build number, app version, locale).

fcmToken.description: FCM token to remove. Typically called on sign-out.

type.description: Catalog type used for deep-link routing.

body.example: Your fare was ₹125.50. Tap to rate your driver.

id.description: Address id, unique within the user.

label.description: Short label — HOME, WORK, or a custom name.

lastUsedAt.description: When this location was last used as a pickup or drop.

passwordSet.description: Whether a login password is set. False for legacy OTP-only users.

emailVerified.description: Whether the email has been verified.

scheduledAt.description: When the account is permanently removed (30-day grace).

registrationNumber.description: India registration number. Letters/digits/hyphens, 5-20 chars. Stored upper-cased.

seatCount.description: Total seats including driver. 1-10 for MVP.

displayName.example: Park Street, Kolkata, West Bengal, 700016, India

polyline.description: Google-encoded polyline (precision 5) of the full route.

baseFare.description: Flat base fare from the active pricing rule (paise).

platformFee.description: Platform fee, paise. Computed on max(subtotal, minimum_fare).

estimatedFare.description: Same as `breakdown.total` — paise the rider will be quoted.
Final rider-facing fare for this type, paise.

options.description: One entry per vehicle type that has an active pricing rule, in BIKE/AUTO/CNG/CAR order. ETA-to-pickup is not included here — it is computed against live drivers at request time.

pickupLocation.description: Where the rider wants to be picked up.

notes.description: Free-text note for the driver (e.g. "Bring helmet"). Shown on the offer.

matchedTrip.description: Populated once a driver accepts (status MATCHED) — drives the rider matched screen.

createdAt.description: ISO timestamp the request was created.

tripPublicId.description: Public id of the created trip.

startOtp.description: Trip start OTP. Only returned to the rider, and only before the trip starts — the rider shows it to the driver, who enters it on start.

actualDistance.description: Actual distance travelled (metres), measured from the ping polyline on end.

actualDuration.description: Actual trip duration (seconds), started_at→ended_at, set on end.

totalFare.description: Final fare, paise. Null until the trip ends.

driverRating.description: Driver rating (1–5) given by the rider.

riderRating.description: Rider rating (1–5) given by the driver.

otp.description: Trip start OTP shown in the rider app. Optional in Sprint 5 Feature 1; enforced once OTP generation lands (Feature 4).

reason.description: Free-text cancellation reason, stored on the trip.

description.example: Driver took a long detour and was rude.
Driver took a long detour and was rude.

savedMethodId.description: Saved payment method to charge (display only — gateway picks on the sheet).

gatewayOrderId.description: Razorpay order id for the SDK

razorpayPaymentId.description: Razorpay payment id from the client SDK

razorpaySignature.description: HMAC-SHA256 signature returned by the Razorpay SDK

driverEarning.description: Net credited to the driver, paise

label.description: Display label — UPI VPA, or a masked card like "•••• 4242". Never raw PAN.

gatewayToken.description: Gateway token from tokenization (optional in dev).

balanceAfter.description: Wallet balance after this entry, paise.

notes.description: Optional note for the finance team.

reference.description: Bank/UPI reference for a PAID payout.

notes.example: AC car, no smoking. Door pickup near Park Street.

routeMatchMeters.description: Search only: straight-line metres from your route ends to the trip ends.

notes.example: AC car, no smoking. Door pickup near Park Street.

scheduledTripId.description: Scheduled trip this thread is about.

mine.description: True if the current user sent this message.

contactsNotified.description: Emergency contacts the alert SMS reached.

recipientPhones.description: Phone numbers to SMS the tracking link to.

recipientsNotified.description: Recipients the link was SMSed to.

lastLocation.description: Last known driver position, if any.

description.example: My UPI was debited ₹250 but the app shows ₹125.

tripId.description: Link the ticket to a trip.

isStaff.description: True if posted by support staff.

subject.example: Left my phone in the auto

description.example: Black iPhone 14, in the back seat.

title.example: How do I book a ride?

body.example: Open the app, enter your destination…
